description: Count of strings matching malicious indicators.
Count of imports from known malicious API list.
Count of network-related API and string indicators.
Shannon entropy of entire file. >7.0 indicates packing/encryption.
Entropy of PE resource section. High = encrypted resources.
Entropy of import library names.
File size in kilobytes.
Total imported functions across all DLLs.
Number of exported functions.
Count of printable ASCII strings ≥4 characters.
Mean entropy across all PE sections.
Proportion of file after last section end. High = appended payload.
Count of registry manipulation API references.
Number of PE sections. Packed files typically have 2–3.
Count of anti-debugging API references.
Number of PE sections with entropy > 7.0.
Binary flag: debug directory present (malware rarely has this).
Binary flag: PE header timestamp is zeroed, future-dated, or epoch.
Binary flag: UPX stub, known packer PE anomalies present.
Binary flag: UPX! magic bytes detected.
